extra.reasoning: rec_drug_a becomes true only via r4 (priority 50), which requires all eight conjuncts true, including low_ejection_fraction. But low_ejection_fraction=true also fires r2 (priority 50), setting rec_drug_b:=true. The two rules write to different outputs (rec_drug_a vs rec_drug_b), so priority-based conflict resolution never suppresses either one. Therefore any input that triggers rec_drug_a inevitably also triggers rec_drug_b, violating the mutual-exclusion property. Concrete witness: set all eight r4 conjuncts (low_ejection_fraction, hypertension, hepatic_impairment, pregnancy, renal_impairment, hyperkalemia, peptic_ulcer, heart_failure) to true; qt_prolongation and elderly are irrelevant (set false). Then r4 sets rec_drug_a=true and r2 sets rec_drug_b=true, so (rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b) holds.
